fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arizona Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arizona - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity arizona, how to transfer audiologist license to arizona, audiologist compact arizona, arizona audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Arizona Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.mailing_address: 1740 West Adams Street, Suite 3006, Phoenix, AZ 85007

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Arizona.

steps.title: Create Account
steps.description: Register for an account on the Arizona State Licensing Portal at licensing.az.gov.

steps.title: Start Application
steps.description: Log in to the Arizona State Licensing Portal, select the Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology, and initiate a new Audiologist License application.

steps.title: Complete & Upload
steps.description: Fill out the online application form and upload all required supporting documents (e.g., transcripts, practicum verification) directly into the Online Application System.

steps.title: Pay Fees
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee and the initial license fee through the Online Application System.

steps.title: Submit Transcripts/Scores
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verification of practicum, and national exam scores (Praxis) to be sent directly to the Board by the issuing institutions.

steps.title: Board Review
steps.description: The Board will review your complete application and notify you of your license status or any further requirements.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Arizona audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Arizona have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Arizona. To work in Arizona, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Arizona?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Arizona
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Arizona, you must submit your application through the Arizona State Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology's Online Application System, accessible via licensing.az.gov.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.portal_name: Arizona State Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology Online Application System
official_links.registration_steps: To apply for an Audiologist license in Arizona, navigate to licensing.az.gov. Click 'Create Account' in the top right corner, then follow the prompts to register with your email and chosen password. Once your account is verified and you are logged in, select 'Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology' from the agency list and choose to start a 'New Application' for an 'Audiologist License'.
